page: Amazon Web Services m7g.medium is a general-purpose instance powered by a custom AWS Graviton3 processor. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features a single dedicated physical core running at 2.6 GHz with a Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-threaded and multi-threaded performance. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests place the instance in the average tier, reflecting the efficiency of the Graviton3 core. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU, multi-core benchmarks, such as multi-core Geekbench and stress-ng, fall into the bottom decile. Memory bandwidth tests, including Passmark memory and large-block read/write operations, also rank in the lower percentiles. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware AES acceleration, though other advanced instruction sets like AVX are absent due to the ARM architecture.

From a resource density perspective, the m7g.medium offers a cost-effective entry point to the Graviton3 ecosystem, allowing developers to deploy ARM-native applications without provisioning larger, more expensive instances. The primary tradeoff is the strict limitation of a single execution thread, which constrains throughput for concurrent database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded compilation. Consequently, this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded utility scripts, microservices, and development or testing environments.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton3 processor core built on the ARM64 architecture. Operating at 2.6 GHz, this Neoverse-V1 core is paired with 4.0 GB of memory, a 1 Gbps baseline network speed, and no local storage. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles due to the single-vCPU limitation. The instance offers a cost-effective entry point for ARM-native development, avoiding the expense of larger multi-core configurations. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, microservices, single-threaded utility tasks, and development or testing environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.medium is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 1 dedicated Graviton3 core, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, optimal for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 instance providing a balanced memory footprint for lightweight utility workloads and microservices.
bullet_points: Powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton3 ARM64 processor core
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers average single-core performance for single-threaded tasks
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration
Ideal for lightweight microservices, utility scripts, and development environments
